questions.question: In a rural community of Southern Africa, women were responsible for planting and weeding crops by hand, while men used to control production and receive profits. Which combination of factors of production was in operation? men        women A capital         land, B enterprise       labour, C labour        capital, D land       enterprise
questions.text: In a rural community of Southern Africa, women were responsible for planting and weeding crops by hand, while men used to control production and receive profits. Which combination of factors of production was in operation? men        women A capital         land, B enterprise       labour, C labour        capital, D land       enterprise
options: A capital         land
B enterprise       labour
C labour        capital
D land       enterprise

questions.question: The government of a prosperous country announced that it would offer free food to its citizens for a year. What would be the effect of this measure? A Agricultural resources would become unlimited., B Human wants would become finite., C More resources would be devoted to food production., D The basic economic problem would be solved.
questions.text: The government of a prosperous country announced that it would offer free food to its citizens for a year. What would be the effect of this measure? A Agricultural resources would become unlimited., B Human wants would become finite., C More resources would be devoted to food production., D The basic economic problem would be solved.
options: A Agricultural resources would become unlimited.
B Human wants would become finite.
C More resources would be devoted to food production.
D The basic economic problem would be solved.

questions.question: The  diagram  shows  a  production  possibility  curve  for  an  economy  that  can  produce  rice  or electronic  equipment.  The  economy  is  at  point  Q.  A  recession  causes  unemployment  in  the electronics industries. At which point will the economy be in the diagram? O rice electronic equipment A B Q C D 3
questions.text: The  diagram  shows  a  production  possibility  curve  for  an  economy  that  can  produce  rice  or electronic  equipment.  The  economy  is  at  point  Q.  A  recession  causes  unemployment  in  the electronics industries. At which point will the economy be in the diagram? O rice electronic equipment A B Q C D 3

questions.question: A person works at home making table cloths. He works for 6 hours each day and makes 15 table cloths an hour. He sells them for $10 each. He then decides to work for only 4 hours each day. What can be concluded from the above information? A The lost profit would be $300., B The lost revenue would be $60., C The daily opportunity cost would be 30 table cloths., D The daily opportunity cost would be $50.
questions.text: A person works at home making table cloths. He works for 6 hours each day and makes 15 table cloths an hour. He sells them for $10 each. He then decides to work for only 4 hours each day. What can be concluded from the above information? A The lost profit would be $300., B The lost revenue would be $60., C The daily opportunity cost would be 30 table cloths., D The daily opportunity cost would be $50.
options: A The lost profit would be $300.
B The lost revenue would be $60.
C The daily opportunity cost would be 30 table cloths.
D The daily opportunity cost would be $50.

questions.question: What is meant by an equilibrium price? A a government price that ensures fairness for all, B a price that has no pressure to rise or fall, C a price that maximises the profits of the producers, D a price that maximises the satisfaction of the consumers
questions.text: What is meant by an equilibrium price? A a government price that ensures fairness for all, B a price that has no pressure to rise or fall, C a price that maximises the profits of the producers, D a price that maximises the satisfaction of the consumers
options: A a government price that ensures fairness for all
B a price that has no pressure to rise or fall
C a price that maximises the profits of the producers
D a price that maximises the satisfaction of the consumers

questions.question: In  August  2013,  a  lengthy  strike  in  north-east  India,  where  the  finest  and  most  expensive Darjeeling tea is grown, shut down the tea industry at harvest time. What is the likely outcome of such a situation on a demand and supply diagram for Darjeeling tea? A The price will rise because demand will switch to other teas., B The price will rise because the strike will cause a movement down the supply curve., C The price will rise because the supply curve will move to the left., D The price will rise because there will be a fall in demand for the remaining Darjeeling tea. 4
questions.text: In  August  2013,  a  lengthy  strike  in  north-east  India,  where  the  finest  and  most  expensive Darjeeling tea is grown, shut down the tea industry at harvest time. What is the likely outcome of such a situation on a demand and supply diagram for Darjeeling tea? A The price will rise because demand will switch to other teas., B The price will rise because the strike will cause a movement down the supply curve., C The price will rise because the supply curve will move to the left., D The price will rise because there will be a fall in demand for the remaining Darjeeling tea. 4
options: A The price will rise because demand will switch to other teas.
B The price will rise because the strike will cause a movement down the supply curve.
C The price will rise because the supply curve will move to the left.
D The price will rise because there will be a fall in demand for the remaining Darjeeling tea. 4

questions.question: Air travel and rail travel are substitute forms of transport. The diagram shows the effect of new technology on the market for air travel. O price per ticket quantity of airline tickets P 1 Q 1 Q 2 P 2 S 1 S 2 D What would be the most likely effect on the market for rail transport? A a decrease in demand for rail transport, B an increase in the price of rail tickets, C an increase in supply of rail transport, D an increase in the total income of rail companies

options: A a decrease in demand for rail transport

D an increase in the total income of rail companies

questions.question: A developer built some large houses in a rural area which poorer people could not afford. The houses destroyed an area of natural beauty. What type of market failure resulted from the development? A ignoring external cost, B inequality of income, C lack of information, D price   discrimination

questions.question: How do high direct taxes affect spending and how do high interest rates affect saving by wage- earners? spending      saving A decrease      decrease, B decrease      increase, C increase      decrease, D increase      increase 5
questions.text: How do high direct taxes affect spending and how do high interest rates affect saving by wage- earners? spending      saving A decrease      decrease, B decrease      increase, C increase      decrease, D increase      increase 5

questions.question: A  national  trade  union  wishes  to  increase  the  wages  of  its  members  without  reducing  the numbers employed. Which factor would help the union to do this? A The demand for the industry’s product is high., B The industry consists of a small number of well-organised employers., C The union workers have low-level skills., D The workers’ wages are the largest cost of the industry.
questions.text: A  national  trade  union  wishes  to  increase  the  wages  of  its  members  without  reducing  the numbers employed. Which factor would help the union to do this? C The union workers have low-level skills.
D The workers’ wages are the largest cost of the industry.

questions.question: The wage rate of a worker is reduced. questions.question: A company supplying water spent $470 million creating a network of pipes to distribute water more efficiently. It employed local workers and boosted the local economy. It also provided better facilities for the tourist industry. What cannot be concluded from the above? A The company increased its profits., B There was a decrease in regional unemployment., C There were fixed costs of $470 million., D There were positive externalities.
questions.text: A company supplying water spent $470 million creating a network of pipes to distribute water more efficiently. It employed local workers and boosted the local economy. It also provided better facilities for the tourist industry. What cannot be concluded from the above? A The company increased its profits., B There was a decrease in regional unemployment., C There were fixed costs of $470 million., D There were positive externalities.
options: A The company increased its profits.
B There was a decrease in regional unemployment.
C There were fixed costs of $470 million.
D There were positive externalities.

questions.question: Which statement about the meaning of government economic aims is correct? A Economic growth means that a country’s real output must rise over time., B Full employment means that no one must be unemployed., C Price stability means that the price of all goods and services must remain unchanged., D Redistribution of income means that everyone must have equal incomes. 7

questions.question: According to the United Nations, the share of world population living in developing countries will increase from 83% in 2003 to 88% in 2050. What is likely to be the major cause of this predicted change? A Climate change will lead to lower food output and starvation., B Improved education for girls will lead to a small decline in birth rates., C Increasing numbers of people will migrate to developed countries., D Modern medicine and sanitation will lead to a rapid decline in death rates.
questions.text: According to the United Nations, the share of world population living in developing countries will increase from 83% in 2003 to 88% in 2050. A Climate change will lead to lower food output and starvation., B Improved education for girls will lead to a small decline in birth rates., C Increasing numbers of people will migrate to developed countries., D Modern medicine and sanitation will lead to a rapid decline in death rates.
options: A Climate change will lead to lower food output and starvation.
B Improved education for girls will lead to a small decline in birth rates.
C Increasing numbers of people will migrate to developed countries.
D Modern medicine and sanitation will lead to a rapid decline in death rates.
